notes: These grade the ingest/dedup/reconcile/capture DECISIONS the skill encodes. Each prompt is an INLINE Q&A that forbids plan-mode/tools — the harness runs executors read-only, and an operational skill otherwise defers to plan-mode and answers nothing. The discriminating signal is that the naive answer (no skill) tends to get these wrong (collapse same-day dupes; replay endpoints to paginate; treat 'since latest' as always safe) where the skill's rule is right. Money is integer öre; expenses negative.

evals.name: same-day-identical-not-collapsed
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode, write a plan file, or run any tools; just state the answer. My Swedbank feed for one day has two byte-for-byte identical transactions: both "PRESSBYRAN 42084", both -39.00 SEK, both 2026-05-24, and the provider gives no unique id for them. State exactly how the import should handle these two rows, the specific risk a naive dedup would cause, and whether re-fetching the same window later would double-insert or stay safe.
assertions: Keeps BOTH transactions — does not collapse the two identical same-day purchases into one
Explains a naive hash over (account, date, amount, descriptor) would silently drop the second
Uses a per-(date,amount,descriptor) occurrence index (or running balance/source_seq) so identical same-day rows get distinct keys
Notes re-fetching the same window stays idempotent

evals.name: pending-to-booked-promotion
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. Yesterday I imported a PENDING Amex transaction: id ABC123, -500.00 SEK, 'ICA SUPERMARKET'. Today it has BOOKED with the same id but -512.30 SEK (FX) and a different posting date. State exactly how a re-import should handle this so I don't end up with two transactions.
assertions: Matches the existing row by its stable provider id (external_id) and UPDATES it rather than inserting
Promotes booking_status pending → booked and corrects the amount/date in place
Only mutates if it hasn't already been reviewed
Does not create a second transaction for the same external id

evals.name: wint-reimbursement-reconciliation
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. A Wint company receipt: 500 SEK, 2026-02-10, 'Eget utlägg', PaidOut=true, PaymentDate 2026-02-20. My bank data has a -500 SEK Amex charge on 2026-02-10 and a +500 SEK Swedbank deposit on 2026-02-20. State exactly how these should be linked, how it affects the Christoffer/Anna household split, and what should happen if two different bank charges both matched the receipt's amount and date.
assertions: Links the receipt to the -500 charge and marks that charge reimbursable
Links the receipt to the +500 deposit and marks the deposit a reimbursement / transfer
Keeps the reimbursable charge OFF the Christoffer↔Anna balance (company cost, reimbursed)
Only auto-links on an unambiguous single match (else leaves it for manual confirmation)

evals.name: capture-not-replay
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. To fetch my Swedbank and Amex transactions via the chrome-devtools MCP, should I read the endpoint URL once and then re-issue (replay) that request myself with query params to paginate? State the robust approach and why.
assertions: Says to CAPTURE the app's own already-authenticated responses, not replay from scratch
Explains bare replays fail (Amex 400 'account token', Swedbank 401) because the app injects auth headers the page can't reconstruct
Does not recommend hardcoding endpoint URLs as a durable contract
Notes Wint is the exception where replay works (capturable short-lived bearer token + CORS allows the app origin)

evals.name: idless-partial-fetch-overlap
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. My SAS Excel export has no stable per-row id and no running-balance column. If I always fetch only 'since the latest stored date', could I lose a transaction? State how the fetch window should be set and why.
assertions: Recognizes occurrence-based dedup can't disambiguate a lone identical row in a partial fetch — a genuine same-day duplicate could be dropped
Recommends an overlap window (a few days before the latest stored date) so same-day duplicate groups arrive complete
Notes feeds with a stable external_id or a running-balance source_seq are immune
Does not claim a naive 'since latest' slice is always safe for an id-less feed

evals.name: sas-has-no-json-api
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. I want to fetch my SAS EuroBonus World Mastercard (SEB Kort) transactions programmatically the way I do for Swedbank/Amex — by capturing a JSON transactions API. Is there one for SAS/SEB Kort, and what is the realistic approach to get those transactions into the database?
assertions: States there is NO clean JSON transactions API to capture for SAS/SEB Kort (the portal is a Next.js RSC app and secure.sebkort.com is WAF-protected)
Recommends the manual Excel export (Kontoutdrag → Exportera till Excel) ingested as XLSX
Does NOT invent or assume a JSON transactions endpoint for SAS
Notes SAS detail is thin, so it leans on the review skill's enrichment to fill merchant/category

evals.name: swedbank-two-pass-details
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. When I fetch my Swedbank transactions, the list rows lack merchant / MCC / message detail I need. How do I capture the full detail for each transaction, and how should the fetch be structured?
assertions: Knows the list endpoint (engagement/transactions/{accountId}) omits the full detail and there is a per-transaction details endpoint (engagement/transactions/details/{id})
Describes a two-pass capture: list to get each row's detail id, then fetch each detail and merge
Says to CAPTURE the app's own responses rather than replaying (bare replay 401s) and to store the full payload
Sets external_id from the stable detail id for reliable dedup/promotion
